product_overview: NXQ Workstation is an internal control plane for human governance of Dimensional Agents, providing task composition, lifecycle orchestration, telemetry tracking, and human approval controls. This PRD (based on NXQ Workstation document pages 1-3) targets a Phase 1 implementation using Firebase + n8n with persistent Firestore storage.
core_goals: Provide secure Firebase-based authentication (Google Workspace) and role-based access control for human operators.
Enable human-driven task composition and dispatch to AI agents with deterministic identity binding (ACELOGIC Dimensional Agent ID).
Ensure persistent job lifecycle tracking and telemetry stored in Firestore and orchestrated via n8n.
Expose a clear dashboard for real-time monitoring (HUD frames, activity log) and support human governance actions (approve, reject, restore).
Validate identity persistence, traceability, and continuity restore logging during the 72-hour validation objective.

features.name: Task Dispatch
features.description: Compose and launch AI agent tasks from the Task Composer UI; tasks are sent to the backend intake API and persist in Firestore for orchestration by n8n.
user_flows: Navigate to /dashboard -> Click Launch Mission Control button -> Select operation protocol (COO) -> Enter task objective in terminal textarea -> Click Launch Agent Task -> Observe POST /api/jobs/intake success -> See new job appear in activity log with status 'assigned'

Navigate to /dashboard -> Click Launch Mission Control button -> Select operation protocol (Research) -> Leave terminal textarea empty -> Click Launch Agent Task -> See validation error 'Objective required' and task not created

Navigate to /dashboard -> Click Launch Mission Control button -> Select operation protocol (Worker) -> Enter task objective in terminal textarea -> Click Launch Agent Task -> Observe POST /api/jobs/intake returns 500 -> See error toast 'Failed to create job' and no job appears in activity log
Navigate to /dashboard -> See sign-in prompt (if not authenticated) -> Attempt sign-in via Google Workspace -> Cancel or fail sign-in -> See access denied message and cannot access Task Composer

features.name: Job Monitoring
features.description: Real-time dashboard showing HUD frames (stats), activity log updates, and detailed telemetry for each job to track lifecycle states and validation metrics.
user_flows: Navigate to /dashboard -> View stats in HUD frames -> Observe real-time job status changes in activity log -> Click job entry in activity log -> See detailed telemetry and lifecycle statuses update to 'in_progress' then 'completed' -> Observe GET /api/dashboard/stats reflect updated success percentage

Navigate to /dashboard -> View stats in HUD frames -> Observe no real-time updates in activity log -> See notification 'Real-time updates unavailable' -> Dashboard falls back to periodic polling and GET /api/dashboard/stats shows stale data until real-time recovers
Navigate to /dashboard -> Click job entry in activity log -> See telemetry section display 'No telemetry available' -> Open Firestore logs and observe missing telemetry entries recorded for that job

features.name: Governance Actions
features.description: Human operators can open job traces to approve, reject (with reason), or restore failed jobs; actions call the governance APIs and update job status and telemetry.
user_flows: Navigate to /dashboard -> Observe activity log -> Open job trace detail -> Click Approve Result -> Observe POST /api/jobs/[job_id]/approve success -> See confirmation and job status updated to 'approved' in activity log

Navigate to /dashboard -> Open job trace detail -> Click Reject Result and provide reason -> Enter rejection reason -> Confirm rejection -> Observe POST /api/jobs/[job_id]/reject success -> See job status updated to 'rejected' and rejection reason recorded in telemetry

Navigate to /dashboard -> Open job trace detail for a failed job -> Click Execute Continuity Restore Protocol on failed jobs -> Observe POST /api/jobs/[job_id]/resurrect success -> See new continuity_restore_event recorded and job status reset to 'assigned'

Navigate to /dashboard -> Open job trace detail -> Click Approve Result -> Observe POST /api/jobs/[job_id]/approve returns 403 -> See error 'Action forbidden: insufficient permissions' and no status change

Navigate to /dashboard -> Open job trace detail for a completed job -> Click Execute Continuity Restore Protocol on failed jobs -> See validation error 'Continuity Restore allowed only for failed jobs' and the action is not executed

features.name: Task Dispatch
features.description: Create and launch AI agent tasks with specific protocols
user_interactions: Enter task objective in terminal textarea

features.name: Job Monitoring
features.description: Real-time update of task status and telemetry
user_interactions: Observe real-time job status changes in activity log

features.name: Governance Actions
features.description: Approve, reject or resurrect agent results
user_interactions: Click Reject Result and provide reason
Click Execute Continuity Restore Protocol on failed jobs
